description: §6.2 Shape-4 const-derived negative — the SAME decl as decl-needs-initializer-pos but WITH the initializer expression (`const <doubled>: int = @count * 2`). A derived cell supplied with an expression is the canonical Shape-3 form, so E-DECL-NEEDS-INITIALIZER stays silent. This is the missing-expression-vs-supplied-expression contrast.
rationale: §6.2 Shape 3 (Derived, Read-Only): "The RHS is an expression marked with `const`. The cell recomputes whenever its dependencies change." The §6.2 Shape-4 parenthetical (SPEC.md:2213) makes the const-derived no-RHS form an error precisely because the expression is absent; supplying it satisfies Shape 3 and the diagnostic does not fire.
